title: scabiosa tomentosa subsp. grosii
intro: Scabiosa tomentosa subsp. grosii, also known as the woolly scabious or woolly pincushion, is a plant species belonging to the honeysuckle family (Caprifoliaceae) and is native to the Iberian Peninsula. Traditionally, Scabiosa tomentosa subsp. grosii has been used in folk medicine due to its medicinal properties, including its anti-inflammatory and antimicrobial activities. The plant has been associated with several health benefits, such as soothing digestive issues, alleviating respiratory problems, and providing relief from skin irritations. Scabiosa tomentosa subsp. grosii can be prepared into herbal teas, infusions, or tinctures, making it accessible for use in traditional remedies.
